text.title: Audience Testing, Exclusions, Layering, and Advantage+
text.summary: How to test audiences systematically, apply exclusions correctly, layer for precision, and decide when to use Advantage+ vs. manual targeting.

section.title: Testing Framework

rule.statement: Test 3-5 audience types simultaneously with identical creatives and equal budget; identify the lowest-CPA winner before scaling.
rule.rationale: Audiences with different creatives confound results — it is impossible to attribute performance differences to the audience or the ad. Identical ads isolate the audience variable. Equal budget ensures each audience gets a fair trial before the algorithm can over-optimize toward one.

example.note: Audience test structure with identical ads across all ad sets.
example.code:
Campaign: Audience Testing ├── Ad Set: Interest_Tech (same 3 ads) ├── Ad Set: Interest_Startup (same 3 ads) ├── Ad Set: LAL_Purchasers_1% (same 3 ads) ├── Ad Set: LAL_Purchasers_2-3% (same 3 ads) └── Ad Set: Retarget_PricingPage_14d (same 3 ads)
Run 7+ days, minimum $20/day per ad set. Winner = lowest CPA with statistically sufficient conversions.

section.p: Testing progression: Start broad (3-5 types), find winner, expand within winning type, create LALs from best performer, repeat.

section.title: Audience Layering
section.p: Combine targeting dimensions for precision using AND logic in Meta's audience builder. Example for B2B SaaS: Interests in SaaS AND Job Title Founder/CEO AND Behavior Business page admin.

rule.statement: Use AND layering to increase precision only when the resulting audience remains above 500K; otherwise the audience is too narrow for efficient delivery.
rule.rationale: AND layers multiply exclusions. Three layers each removing 70% of the audience leaves 2.7% of the original pool — often below the delivery threshold that causes CPM to spike.

antipattern.description: Layering 4+ interest and behavior dimensions on a small-country audience.
antipattern.reason: The resulting audience may be too small for efficient delivery. Meta requires sufficient scale to find the right users at competitive auction prices.

section.title: Exclusion Strategy

rule.statement: Exclude recent purchasers from all acquisition campaigns; exclude current trial users from trial acquisition campaigns; exclude leads from lead generation campaigns.
rule.rationale: Without exclusions, acquisition campaigns optimize toward existing customers (easiest to convert because they already know the brand), producing inflated conversion metrics that do not represent new customer acquisition.

section.p: Exclusion windows: Purchases — exclude for 30-90 days then upsell. Trial start — exclude until trial ends. Lead form submission — exclude until sales follow-up complete.

rule.statement: Use Advantage+ Audience for large budgets (over $100/day) with broad-appeal products; use manual targeting for niche products, small budgets, or when retargeting specific segments.
rule.rationale: Advantage+ needs scale to work — its AI requires sufficient conversion data to learn effectively. Niche products benefit from human-defined constraints that prevent the algorithm from drifting to irrelevant but easily-reachable users.
